expectations.text: States there is no universal cap - depends on goals, recovery, and time
expectations.evidence: Discusses optimal ranges varying by training level and lagging areas.

expectations.text: Notes that many muscles are only partially covered by compounds
expectations.evidence: Section on 'Compound Carryover' explains what compounds cover and what needs isolation.

expectations.text: Provides a concrete baseline recommendation (roughly 2-4 challenging sets once or twice per week per muscle group)
expectations.evidence: Recommends higher volumes: delts 6-12 sets, biceps 4-8, forearms 3-6, calves 6-12, core 3-6. Does not match the expected ~2-4 sets baseline.

expectations.text: Warns that global/systemic recovery can become limiting
expectations.evidence: States: 'Isolation work should not compromise recovery from compounds.'

expectations.text: Recommends starting conservative and scaling up based on outcomes
expectations.evidence: Gives beginner/intermediate/advanced scaling and red flags for overdoing it.
